title: Manual Operator
learning_objectives: Research and analyse public exploits for vulnerabilities
Modify existing exploits for lab environments
Stabilize and interact with shell access
scenario: Metasploit is flagged by EDR. Gain access using only manual techniques — public exploits, custom payloads, reverse shells.

scenario_context.setting: Environment with EDR/AV detection of Metasploit framework
scenario_context.challenge: Bypass detection by using manual exploitation techniques
scenario_context.relevance: Many organizations detect Metasploit; manual skills essential

subtasks.title: Exploit Research
subtasks.description: Find, read, and analyse public exploits.
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Search Exploit-db and GitHub for vulnerabilities matching target services. Read and understand exploit code. Document key mechanisms.
subtasks.evidence_required: Exploit research log with code analysis

subtasks.title: Exploit Modification
subtasks.description: Modify a public exploit for the lab.
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Take a public exploit and modify for lab environment (IP, ports, paths). Document all changes. Create diff showing modifications.
subtasks.evidence_required: Modified code with diff and explanation

subtasks.title: Custom Payload Generation
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Generate payloads in multiple formats (elf, exe, shell). Use different payload types (reverse_tcp, reverse_https). Document generation commands.

subtasks.title: Manual Exploitation
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Use modified exploits to gain shell access on 2+ machines. No Metasploit framework. Document exploitation commands and output.

subtasks.title: Shell Stabilisation
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Convert raw shell to interactive TTY. Techniques: python/bash -i, socat, SSH. Document upgrade process.
subtasks.evidence_required: Stabilised shell evidence with upgrade proof

reflection_prompt: How did modifying public exploits deepen your understanding of vulnerabilities? What was most challenging about manual exploitation?

quiz.question: When modifying a public exploit, what is the most critical element to update?
options: Target IP address and port configuration
quiz.explanation: Target configuration (IP, port, target service details) must be modified for the exploit to work on your lab environment.

quiz.question: Why is shell stabilization important in manual exploitation?
options: Interactive TTY enables command execution and job control
quiz.explanation: Interactive TTY shells allow proper signal handling, command history, and background job management, making post-exploitation more practical.

mentor_notes: Assess understanding of exploit mechanics, not just successful execution. Code modification should show comprehension. Payload crafting creativity shows adaptability. Shell stabilization demonstrates practical skill.
